configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: Hostname or IP address of the Datadog Agent that this library sends trace data to.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables AI Guard request inspection in the tracer; when disabled, AI Guard checks are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: AI Guard: sets the base endpoint URL for the AI Guard REST API. If unset, defaults to `https://app.<DD_SITE>/api/v2/ai-guard` (the tracer appends `/evaluate`).

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_CONTENT_SIZE
configurations.description: Max size of the content property set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_MESSAGES_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum number of conversational messages allowed to be set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Timeout used in calls to the AI Guard REST API in milliseconds (default 5000)

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: To use the Datadog CLI in Azure Cloud Shell, open a cloud shell, set your API key and site in the `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables, and use `npx` to run the CLI directly:

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_BODY_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Defines the probability of a downstream request body being sampled, or said differently, defines the overall number of requests for which the request and response body should be sampled / analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API Security features are enabled. If unset, API Security is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API endpoint definitions are collected for API catalog features. If unset, endpoint collection is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_MAX_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS
configurations.description: The maximum number of downstream requests per request for which the request and response body should be analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_REQUEST_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sets the sampling rate for API Security schema extraction. Values are clamped to the range 0.0 to 1.0; if unset or invalid, a default rate is used.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_SAMPLE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay (in seconds) between API Security schema samples. This setting is intended for system testing and is not meant for general use.

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTOMATED_USER_EVENTS_TRACKING
configurations.description: Controls automated user events tracking for AppSec, including enabling/disabling it and selecting a tracking mode. Deprecated; prefer the appsec.auto_user_instrumentation.mode setting.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE
configurations.description: Selects the AppSec automatic user instrumentation mode, which controls how user events are collected.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_TEXT
configurations.description: Path to a plain-text template file used for blocked-request responses when plain text is the chosen format. If unset, the tracer uses its default text template or falls back to the JSON/HTML default behavior

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES
configurations.description: Limits how many separate stacktraces are captured and attached to an AppSec/IAST event to keep payload sizes reasonable. Evidence stacktraces are useful for triage, but unbounded capture can leak sensitive data and consume excessive bandwidth; this limit balances evidence value and safety. Set to 0 to collect all.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH_TOP_PERCENT
configurations.description: When the tracer must trim a stacktrace, this value controls how much of the top portion of the stacktrace is preserved at higher fidelity (a percentage). This lets implementations keep more of the top frames (most actionable) while trimming the less usefully deep frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_VALUE_REGEXP
configurations.description: Regular expression used to find and redact sensitive substrings inside parameter values and highlights. Matches are replaced with `<redacted by datadog>`. This regexp complements the key regexp: the key regexp takes precedence; when no key match exists, the value regexp runs. The default pattern targeted at tokens, JWTs, private key blocks, and other credential shapes. Empty string disables the value regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Turns on the tracer's RASP / Exploit Prevention capabilities. When enabled the tracer runs synchronous, pre-execution checks and may generate block actions or exploit signals. RASP may have separate activation and implementation details per language; enabling it usually activates extra synchronous instrumentation and additional evidence capture for detections

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Controls the maximum amount of AppSec traces, per second.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_DEBUG
configurations.description: Turns on internal debug/trace logging for the in-app WAF. This is intended for debugging and is very verbose — it can severely impact performance. Use together with `DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT` when debugging and prefer narrow log filters on production systems

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds)

configurations.name: DD_APP_KEY
configurations.description: Sets the application key used to authenticate LLM Observability dataset and experiment requests in agentless mode. If agentless mode is enabled and this is not set, dataset/experiment operations that require an application key will fail.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Crash Tracking. When enabled, the tracer initializes crash tracking and configures JVM crash/OOM hooks (for example, `-XX:OnError` and `-XX:OnOutOfMemoryError`) to run Datadog-provided scripts that upload `hs_err_pid*.log` crash logs and OOME notifications. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Data Streams Monitoring (DSM) and propagation. When enabled, the tracer registers the DSM propagator and reports pathway stats/payloads. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_INJECT_SQL_BASEHASH
configurations.description: Enables the injection of a "base hash" in SQL queries when DBM propagation is also enabled. This base hash is used to find matching spans, and enrich the queries with related values.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DISABLE_DATADOG_RAILS
configurations.description: Disables the Datadog Rails integration auto-instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Dynamic Instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_PROBE_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a local probe definition JSON file. When set, the tracer loads probe definitions from this file at startup instead of subscribing to remote configuration.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of additional identifier keywords to redact in captured snapshot data

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_TYPES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of object types to redact from dynamic logs and snapshots. Supports * wildcards (e.g. Secret* matches SecretKey).

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTION_EXCLUDED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of identifier keywords to exclude from the default redaction list. Useful to prevent common keywords like password from being treated as sensitive for your use case.

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: The application's environment. Sets the "env" tag on every span.

configurations.name: DD_ERROR_TRACKING_HANDLED_ERRORS
configurations.description: Controls whether Error Tracking reports handled errors from user code, third-party gems, or both.

configurations.name: DD_ERROR_TRACKING_HANDLED_ERRORS_INCLUDE
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of paths, file names, or gem names whose handled errors should be reported by Error Tracking.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the experimental feature-flag provider that evaluates flags from remote configuration. If not enabled, the provider is a no-op and returns default values.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables experimental propagation of process tags in payloads such as spans and telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_EXTERNAL_ENV
configurations.description: Sets an external environment identifier attached to emitted telemetry for origin and environment attribution.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit SHA reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_REPOSITORY_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the git repository URL reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled. Any embedded credentials in the URL are stripped before use.

configurations.name: DD_HEALTH_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables health metrics collection.

configurations.name: DD_INJECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Indicates whether the library was loaded via Datadog single-step instrumentation (SSI) injection.

configurations.name: DD_INJECT_FORCE
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): forces the tracer javaagent to load even when multiple JVM agents are detected. This bypasses SSI guardrails that would otherwise abort startup to avoid running multiple agents. Can be set via `DD_INJECT_FORCE` or system property `dd.inject.force`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_ID
configurations.description: Sets the install ID included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers. This can be used to correlate telemetry to a specific installation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TIME
configurations.description: Sets the install time included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TYPE
configurations.description: Sets the install type included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_INJECTION
configurations.description: **Supported Input**: Boolean (`true`/`false`) **Caveats**: Not supported in C++ or Go. **Description**: Enables or disables the automatic injection of trace context (trace ID, span ID) into JSON/structured application logs. This allows for correlation between traces and logs.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_METRIC_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: Port of the Datadog Agent StatsD endpoint that this library sends metrics to.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ALLOCATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: If set to `true`, enables Allocation profiling (in Preview). Defaults to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_DIR_INTERRUPTION_WORKAROUND_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables a workaround for Ruby Dir interruption issues caused by profiling signals.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable the profiler by setting `-Ddd.profiling.enabled` flag or `DD_PROFILING_ENABLED` environment variable to `true`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether spans add a pprof label with the top-level request endpoint so profiles can be grouped by endpoint. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_HEAP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables preview heap profiling, collecting heap profiles (requires allocation profiling).

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_HEAP_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for heap profiling.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_HEAP_SIZE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables collection of heap size profiles for heap profiling (requires allocation profiling and heap profiling).

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_GC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables garbage collection profiling, collecting GC activity in profiles.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_GVL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables GVL profiling to report time threads spend waiting for the Global VM Lock.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_HEAP_CLEAN_AFTER_GC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether heap profiling performs cleanup after garbage collection, rather than just at serialization time (requires GC profiling and heap profiling).

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_MAX_FRAMES
configurations.description: Maximum number of stack frames captured per thread sample in profiles.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_NATIVE_FILENAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the profiler uses native filenames for stack frames from native code.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_NO_SIGNALS_WORKAROUND_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables a workaround mode that reduces reliance on profiling signals to avoid interrupt-related issues in some libraries.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_OVERHEAD_TARGET_PERCENTAGE
configurations.description: Target overhead percentage for profiling; the profiler adjusts sampling to stay near this target.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_PREVIEW_OTEL_CONTEXT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the profiler attempts to read context from OpenTelemetry.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_SHUTDOWN_ON_EXEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Stops profiling before an exec call to avoid profiling state leaking into the new process.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_SIGHANDLER_SAMPLING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the profiler samples directly from the signal handler for improved accuracy.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_SKIP_MYSQL2_CHECK
configurations.description: Disables the profiler compatibility check of the libmysqlclient version used by mysql2.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_TIMELINE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable the timeline profile type. Added in version `0.89.0`. **Note**: This supersedes the `DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_TIMELINE_ENABLED` environment variable (`datadog.profiling.experimental_timeline_enabled` INI setting), which was available since `0.89` (default `0`). If both are set, this one takes precedence.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_PERIOD
configurations.description: Profiling: upload period in seconds (recording duration); controls how frequently profiles are uploaded and is used to derive per-recording sampling budgets. Default: 60s.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Controls timeout behavior for tracer operations when communicating with external components.

configurations.name: DD_REDIS_COMMAND_ARGS
configurations.description: When enabled, includes Redis command arguments in span resource names and tags; otherwise only the command name is shown.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIGURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable or disable remote configuration. Also accepts the alias `DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
configurations.description: Startup timeout for Remote Configuration; if exceeded, the application proceeds using local configuration.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: **Since**: 0.2.0 Sets how often, in seconds, the Datadog Agent is queried for Remote Configuration updates.

configurations.name: DD_RODA_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Roda integration.

configurations.name: DD_RODA_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Roda integration.

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the collection of runtime metrics (such as garbage collection stats, memory usage, and thread counts) for the application.

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_RUNTIME_ID_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, adds a runtime-id tag to runtime metrics to distinguish individual processes.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE
configurations.description: Your application's default service name, set as the service tag on traces.

configurations.name: DD_SITE
configurations.description: Datadog site used to select the Datadog intake domain for agentless requests, such as telemetry and AI Guard API calls.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Span sampling: JSON rules used to decide per-span sampling priority (single-span sampling). If both `DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES` and `DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE` are set, the file setting is ignored. Rules can match spans by service/name and apply a sample rate and optional max-per-second limit.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE
configurations.description: Points to a JSON file that contains the span sampling rules. DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES takes precedence over this variable. See DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES for the rule format.

configurations.name: DD_TAGS
configurations.description: Custom tags applied to traces, provided as comma-separated key:value pairs (e.g. `layer:api,team:intake`).

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the agentless telemetry intake URL.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_DEPENDENCY_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Telemetry: enables the dependency collection service that detects application dependencies via classloading and reports them. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval for the telemetry status heartbeat (in seconds)

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_LOG_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true : Enable or disable log collection for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_METRICS_AGGREGATION_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Interval at which instrumentation telemetry metrics are aggregated before being sent.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether instrumentation telemetry sends internal metrics. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_GENERATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether new traces use 128-bit W3C trace IDs (32-character hexadecimal strings) or 64-bit Datadog trace IDs (16-character hexadecimal strings). The default is true to support W3C trace context propagation.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_LOGGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether 128-bit trace IDs are logged in their full 32-character format or truncated to 16 characters. Set to false for compatibility with systems that expect the shorter format.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_CABLE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Action Cable integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_CABLE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Action Cable integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_CABLE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Action Cable integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_MAILER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Action Mailer integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_MAILER_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Action Mailer integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_MAILER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Action Mailer integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_PACK_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Action Pack integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_PACK_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Action Pack integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_PACK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Action Pack integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_VIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Action View integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_VIEW_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Action View integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTION_VIEW_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Action View integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_JOB_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Active Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_JOB_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Active Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_JOB_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Active Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_MODEL_SERIALIZERS_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Active Model Serializers integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_MODEL_SERIALIZERS_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Active Model Serializers integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_MODEL_SERIALIZERS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Active Model Serializers integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_RECORD_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Active Record integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_RECORD_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Active Record integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_RECORD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Active Record integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_SUPPORT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Active Support integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_SUPPORT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Active Support integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVE_SUPPORT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Active Support integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the Trace Agent that the tracer submits to. If the Agent configuration sets receiver_port or DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT to something other than the default 8126, then DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT or DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL must match it.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
configurations.description: Timeout (in seconds) for connecting to the Datadog Agent when sending trace data.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL
configurations.description: URL endpoint where this library sends trace data to the Datadog Agent. Can use HTTP or a Unix domain socket. Has priority over `agent.host` and `agent.port`. If the Agent configuration sets `receiver_port` or `DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT` to something other than the default `8126`, then `DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT` or `DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the legacy tracing analytics feature (deprecated).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the AWS SDK integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the AWS integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the AWS integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_PEER_SERVICE
configurations.description: Name of the external service for spans generated by the AWS integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the AWS integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_TAG_KEYS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of baggage keys to copy into span tags. Set to * to tag all baggage items; if unset, a default allowlist is used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the automatic collection of client IP addresses from HTTP request headers. When enabled, the IP address is stored in the http.client_ip span tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER
configurations.description: Name of an optional custom HTTP header used to resolve the client IP address.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CONCURRENT_RUBY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the Concurrent Ruby integration propagates tracing context.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DALLI_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Dalli integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DALLI_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Dalli integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DALLI_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Dalli integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DALLI_PEER_SERVICE
configurations.description: Name of the external service for spans generated by the Dalli integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DALLI_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the Dalli integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG
configurations.description: Enable debug logging in the tracer. Use OTEL_LOG_LEVEL as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DELAYED_JOB_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Delayed Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DELAYED_JOB_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Delayed Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DELAYED_JOB_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Delayed Job integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTICSEARCH_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Elasticsearch integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTICSEARCH_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Elasticsearch integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLED

configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the Elasticsearch integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables tracing. When disabled, instrumentation still runs but no trace data is sent.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ETHON_ANALYTICS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXCON_ANALYTICS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXCON_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Excon integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXCON_ERROR_STATUS_CODES
configurations.description: HTTP status codes that should be marked as errors for spans generated by the Excon integration. Value can be a range (`400...600`), or an array of ranges/integers `[403, 500...600]`. If configured with environment variable, use dash for range (`'400-599'`) and comma for adding element into an array (`'403,500-599'`)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXCON_PEER_SERVICE
configurations.description: Name of the external service for spans generated by the Excon integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXCON_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the Excon integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXPERIMENTAL_RUNTIME_ID_ENABLED

configurations.description: Specifies which GraphQL error extensions to capture. A comma-separated list of extension keys to capture. Empty or not present means no extensions are captured.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_ERROR_TRACKING

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_WITH_UNIFIED_TRACER

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HANAMI_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAGS
configurations.description: Automatically apply specified HTTP headers as span tags. If a custom tag name is not specified, the tag key defaults to http.request.headers. for request headers and http.response.headers. for response headers.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for http. When disabled, spans for http operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_ERROR_STATUS_CODES
configurations.description: HTTP status codes that should be marked as errors for spans generated by the net/http instrumentation. Value can be a range (`400...600`), or an array of ranges/integers `[403, 500...600]`. If configured with environment variable, use dash for range (`'400-599'`) and comma for adding element into an array (`'403,500-599'`)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default 5xx status codes are reported as errors for http servers. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.trace.http.server.error.statuses=500,502-599`

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_MAPPING

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_EXTRACT_FIRST
configurations.description: When set to true, stops extracting after the first successful trace context extraction.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE
configurations.description: Distributed tracing propagation formats to use for both extraction and injection.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT
configurations.description: comma-separated list of header formats to include to propagate distributed traces between services.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_QUE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_QUE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_QUE_TAG_ARGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tagging a Que job's args field on spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_QUE_TAG_DATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tagging a Que job's data field on spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RAILS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RAKE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Rake integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RAKE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Rake integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RAKE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Rake integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of traces to sample per second; applies only when either DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES or DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE is set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by Redis integrations by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Redis integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for redis. When disabled, spans for redis operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_PEER_SERVICE
configurations.description: Name of the external service for spans generated by the Redis integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the Redis integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, disables inferred-services/integration service naming by making Naming Schema v0 not allow inferred services (so integrations that rely on inferred services fall back to application service naming)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REPORT_HOSTNAME
configurations.description: Whether to report the system's hostname for each trace. When disabled, the hostname of the Agent is used instead.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ALWAYS_SIMPLIFIED_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Always set the http.endpoint tag with the results of the simplification algorithm of Resource Renaming

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the http.endpoint tag is computed and added for incoming HTTP requests. This is disabled by default unless application security is enabled at startup, and can be explicitly enabled or disabled with this setting.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESQUE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the Resque integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESQUE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Resque integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESQUE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Resque integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REST_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the RestClient integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REST_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the RestClient integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REST_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the RestClient integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REST_CLIENT_PEER_SERVICE

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RODA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Roda integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the ingestion sample rate (between 0.0 and 1.0) between the Agent and the backend. Use OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Semi-colon separated list of sampling rules. The rule is matched in order of specification. The first match in a list is used. Per entry: The item "sample_rate" is required in decimal format. The item "service" is optional in regular expression format, to match on service name. The item "name" is optional in regular expression format, to match on operation name. To give a rate of 50% to any traces in a service starting with the text "cart": '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}]' To give a rate of 20% to any traces which have an operation name of "http.request": '[{"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}]' To give a rate of 100% to any traces within a service named "background" and with an operation name of "sql.query": '[{"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}] To give a rate of 10% to all traces '[{"sample_rate":0.1}]' To configure multiple rules, separate by semi-colon and order from most specific to least specific: '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}, {"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}, {"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}, {"sample_rate":0.1}]' If no rules are specified, or none match, default internal sampling logic will be used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SEMANTIC_LOGGER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables injecting trace correlation fields into Semantic Logger logs when log injection is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SEQUEL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRILOGY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Trilogy integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRILOGY_PEER_SERVICE
configurations.description: Name of the external service for spans generated by the Trilogy integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRILOGY_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Service name used for spans generated by the Trilogy integration. May be overridden by `DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WATERDROP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the WaterDrop integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Your application version, set as the version tag on traces.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Specifies the base URL for sending OTLP data for all signals unless overridden. **Default (gRPC)**: `http://localhost:4317`. **Default (HTTP)**: `http://localhost:4318`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set custom headers for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific headers). Used when metrics-specific OTLP headers are not set. Format: api-key=key,other=value.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: If non-empty, used as the OTLP metrics endpoint. Otherwise falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, then to `http://<DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL host>:4317/4318` based on protocol. For `http/protobuf`, `/v1/metrics` is appended if missing.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of key-value pairs to be used as headers on outgoing OTLP metrics requests (for example, `api-key=key,other-config=value`). Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol to use for metrics data. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Selects the protocol used for OpenTelemetry OTLP metrics export. Only http/protobuf is supported.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: Controls metrics aggregation temporality. Accepted values: `delta` or `cumulative` (case-insensitive); empty/invalid values are treated as `delta`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Used as the exporter network timeout (ms) for OTLP metrics requests. If set to `0`, the library falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the timeout (in milliseconds) for a single outgoing OTLP metrics request. Takes precedence over the general OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT. **Default**: 10000 (10s).

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the transport protocol to use for all signals unless overridden. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific timeout). Used when the metrics-specific OTLP timeout is not set. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Enables debug mode, producing verbose tracer logs (not recommended for production or sensitive environments) when set to debug. Alias for DD_TRACE_DEBUG.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Specifies the metrics exporter to be used. **Notes**: The only accepted values are `otlp` and `none`. A value of `none` disables the emission of OTel metrics, as well as APM runtime metrics (equivalent to `DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED=false`)

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) between metric export attempts. **Default**: 10000 (10s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 60000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to collect and export metrics. **Default**: 7500 (7.5s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 30000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Configures which propagators are used for extracting and injecting trace context.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Set OpenTelemetry resource attributes as a list of `(key, value)` pairs.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Your application's default service name, set as the service tag on traces. Alias for DD_SERVICE.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Enables or disables tracing. When disabled, instrumentation still runs but no trace data is sent. Alias for DD_TRACE_ENABLED.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampling rate, controlling the fraction of traces kept. Alias for DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
configurations.description: Sets the argument for OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER when using the traceidratio sampler (for example, OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG=0.25). This is used to derive the tracer sample rate when mapping OpenTelemetry configuration.
